text: Investigate & fix the root cause of the 300s worker busy-timeout that drops the supervisor into ERROR
description:
The supervisor repeatedly enters ERROR via enterError("Worker session stuck (busy timeout reached after 300s)") — the worker shows a busy indicator and never goes idle until MAX_BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS (watchdog-service.ts ~425). Seen twice on 2026-06-21 (14:23, 15:10 UTC) in logs/7000/out.log.

Hypothesis: the worker wedges on an interactive prompt (permission dialog, "continue?", login, pager) that the busy-detection reads as "still working", so it never resolves and only the absolute timeout fires.

Approach:
1. Use the new diagnostics (logs/supervisor-errors.log + the ERROR block in logs/<port>/out.log, from branch watchdog-error-diagnostics) to read the worker terminal tail at the moment of the next timeout and confirm what it was waiting on.
2. If it's an interactive prompt: detect that state and auto-respond/interrupt (cf. the existing Gemini double-ESC interrupt action, aim bc0a18cf) rather than waiting the full 300s, or escalate to the watchdog earlier.
3. Consider distinguishing "genuinely working" from "blocked on input" in isAgentEffectivelyBusy so a wedged prompt doesn't read as busy.

Done when the cause is confirmed from a real capture and the worker either recovers automatically or the supervisor escalates well before the 300s hard timeout.

reflections.context: The supervisor kept entering ERROR with 'Worker session stuck (busy timeout reached after 300s)'. Initial hypothesis was an interactive prompt wedging the worker.
reflections.outcome: The diagnostics log disproved the hypothesis and revealed two distinct bugs: the busy timer never reset on idle (measured wall-clock since enable), and it was a hard cap on continuous work (killed a worker that was actively producing output for 304s). Fixed both (reset on idle; reset on screen-change/progress, trip only on a frozen screen), extracted the logic into a tested helper, plus diagnostics + auto-retry.
reflections.effectiveness: High once the diagnostics existed. The earlier fixes looked ineffective only because the long-running session process never hot-reloads (--import tsx, no watch) — every fix needed a manual session restart to deploy. Reading the actual worker terminal tail at the moment of error is what cracked it.
reflections.lesson: Don't trust a plausible hypothesis (interactive prompt) over evidence — instrument first, then read the real capture. And a duration-based 'stuck' heuristic is wrong for capable agents: distinguish frozen (static screen) from working (changing screen), never kill by elapsed time alone. Also: dev agent sessions don't hot-reload, so watchdog fixes require a session restart to take effect.

status.comment: Done. Root cause was TWO failure modes, both confirmed from logs/supervisor-errors.log (the diagnostics built for this), not the interactive-prompt hypothesis: (1) busyStartedAt never reset on idle → timer measured wall-clock-since-enable (fix dedaef6: reset on idle); (2) timer was a hard cap on CONTINUOUS work → a worker legitimately working >300s was killed (capture showed the worker actively producing output for 304s). Fix cda9f31: reset the timer on any screen change (output/ticking spinner = progress); only a frozen screen trips it. Refactored the decision into advanceWorkerBusyTimeout() + 4 regression tests (5901de4). Also shipped: ERROR-state diagnostics (f3c8513) and 10-min auto-retry (abc4273). All on main. Live-confirmed: this session worked many minutes continuously without a false ERROR.
